# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for our Python Flask API. The app needs to install dependencies from requirements.txt, expose port 5000, and run the Flask application. Keep it simple and straightforward for quick deployment to production.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official lightweight python runtime as a parent image (focal)
FROM tiangolo/uwsgi-nginx-flask  # change this if necessary, base should be the version you want flask app to run on. You can use focal for Python3 compatibility and latest tag of uWSGI service or whatever is available in Docker registry by default
ARG FLASK_APP=app.py         # Specify your Flask application file as an argument (default value) 
WORKDIR /home/uwsgi-nginx   # Set work directory to a safe place, if necessary for the container's files and folders (- workingdir in uWSGI default location). Change it according. It will be where all of your file get copied during building docker image (app & nginx)
COPY requirements.txt .     	# Copy requirement here so that flask can install dependencies into a virtualenv format via pip freeze, which is ideal for deployment to production server 	
RUN pip3 install -r requirements.txt    # Install python dependences mentioned in the file using pip and make sure you use latest stable version (pip3) of all packages because we specified Python as runtime environment by ARG command below before docker build . This will ensure that our Flask app is running on a compatible system for production deployment 
EXPOSE 5000              # Expose the application to allow connections outside this container from any port, in case you need access via web browser or other service. You can map different ports as needed by changing EXPOSE line (if necessary) before docker build . In our setup it's already set up for simplicity
ENTRYPOINT ["uwsgi", "--ini=/home/flask-config.ini"]  # The command the container will run when starting, should match what is specified in INI file to serve your app (default location of flask config). In our setup it's already set up for simplicity
